## Building a Search Bar with History in Vue 3 **Mental model:** On submit, push the query to a recent list — most-recent-first, de-duplicated, capped. A dropdown shows history on focus; clicking an item re-runs it. Persist to `localStorage` in a real app. ### Step 1 — Query + history ```js const query = ref(''); const history = ref(['react hooks', 'css grid']); ``` ### Step 2 — Add on submit ```js function submit(q) { const t = q.trim(); if (!t) return; history.value = [t, ...history.value.filter((x) => x !== t)].slice(0, MAX); } ``` ### Step 3 — Show history on focus ```vue
  • {{ h }}
``` ### Step 4 — Reuse + clear ```js function reuse(q) { query.value = q; submit(q); } ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Duplicate | Filter then re-add at front. | | Overflow | `slice(0, MAX)`. | | Click vs blur | `@mousedown` beats `@blur`. | **Interview tip:** `@mousedown` (not `@click`) on history items is the detail — it fires before the input's `@blur` hides the list, so the selection registers.

angular.answer:
## Building a Search Bar with History in Angular **Mental model:** On submit, push the query to a recent list — most-recent-first, de-duplicated, capped. A dropdown shows history on focus; `mousedown` on an item re-runs it (beating the input blur). Persist to `localStorage` in a real app. Classic `NgModule` setup, no `?.`/`??` (the build rejects them), no `FormsModule`. ### Step 1 — Fields ```typescript query = ''; history: string[] = ['angular signals', 'rxjs operators']; focused = false; ``` ### Step 2 — Add on submit ```typescript submit(q: string): void { const t = q.trim(); if (!t) { return; } this.history = [t, ...this.history.filter((x) => x !== t)].slice(0, this.MAX); } ``` ### Step 3 — Show on focus ```html
  • {{ h }}
``` ### Step 4 — Reuse + clear ```typescript reuse(q: string): void { this.query = q; this.submit(q); } ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Duplicate | Filter then re-add at front. | | Overflow | `slice(0, MAX)`. | | Click vs blur | `(mousedown)` beats `(blur)`. | **Interview tip:** Bind `(mousedown)` (not `(click)`) on the history rows so selection fires before the input's `(blur)` closes the dropdown — the same blur/click race as autocomplete.
